text:
A patrol vessel matches your heading as you approach the docking ring. Standard challenge — transponder verification, cargo manifest request, the usual. But the officer's tone has an edge to it. More thorough than routine.

"Independent vessel, confirm your registration and purpose of visit. We're running enhanced security protocols in this sector."

Your comms officer looks at you. How you handle this sets a tone.

choices.label: Full cooperation — transmit everything they ask for
choices.tone_note: Compliance. Nothing to hide, nothing to fear.

effects.text: Complied fully with the security check. The officer seemed satisfied.

choices.label: Comply, but ask what the enhanced protocols are about
choices.tone_note: Cooperative but curious. Information is currency.

effects.text: Passed inspection. The officer mentioned increased frontier activity but wouldn't elaborate.

choices.label: Transmit the minimum required by law
choices.tone_note: Guarded. Privacy matters, even from authorities.

effects.text: Provided minimum documentation. The officer noted it but cleared you through.

text:
Docking protocols complete. The station assigns you a berth and a local frequency. Standard automated welcome — berth number, emergency procedures, a reminder that weapons must be secured while aboard.

The formality of it is almost comforting. Civilization has procedures. Procedures mean someone is maintaining order, however thinly.

effects.text: Checked the station directory. Services, contacts, the usual.
